isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t        try:\n246\t            obj = json.loads(data)\n247\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n248\t            return [('value', data)]\n249\t\n250\t    # Handle array\n251\t    if isinstance(obj, list):\n252\t        rows = []\n253\t        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n254\t            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n255\t        return rows\n256\t\n257\t    # _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n262\t        return rows\n263\t\n264\t    # Handle primitive\n265\t    return [('value', str(obj))]\n266\t\n267\t\n268\tdef render_fallback_card(\n269\t    summary: str | It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n346\t    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n347\t\n348\t    Args:\n349\t        result_type: The result type to match no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\ndef select_rendered_card(\n    result_type: str,\n    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n    result_id: str,\n    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic component selector: (result_type, result.data) → Optional[rendered_html].\n\n    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    # Query component_usage_patterns for highest match_score where result_type matches\n    component = lib.select_component_for_result_type(\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_threshold=match_threshold,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    # If no component matched or below threshold, return None (triggers fallback)\n    if component is None:\n        logger.debug(\n            \"select_rendered_card: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n            \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n            result_type,\n            match_threshold,\n            result_id,\n        )\n        return None\n\n    # On match: fill component template with result.data\n    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n    # Write to card_cache\n    lib.cache_card(\n        result_id=result_id,\n        component_id=component.id,\n        component_version=component.version,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n    )\n\n    # Record usage pattern (hot-path match is high confidence, so score=1.0)\n    lib.record_usage_pattern(\n        component_id=component.id,\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_score=1.0,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    logger.info(\n        \"select_rendered_card: rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n        result_id,\n        component.id,\n        component.version,\n        result_type,\n    )\n\n    return rendered_html\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":431,"startLine":1,"totalLines":431}}}

        \"\"\"\n201\t            INSERT INTO components (id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, usage_count)\n202\t            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?, 0)\n203\t            \"\"\",\n204\t            (component_id, name, description, html_template, created_at)\n205\t        )\n206\t\n207\t        # Create version history\n208\t        conn.execute(\n209\t            \"\"\"\n210\t            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n211\t            VALUES (?, 1, ?, ?, ?)\n212\t            \"\"\",\n213\t            (component_id, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n214\t        )\n215\t\n216\t        conn.commit()\n217\t\n218\t        return Component(\n219\t            id=component_id,\n220\t ID.\"\"\"\n231\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n232\t        row = conn.execute(\n233\t            \"SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count \"\n234\t            \"FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n235\t            (component_id,)\n236\t        ).fetchone()\n237\t\n238\t        if row:\n239\t            return Component(\n240\t  self,\n253\t        result_type: str,\n254\t        result_data: Dict[str, Any],\n255\t        layout_bucket: str = 'normal'\n256\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n257\t        \"\"\"\n258\t        Find the best-fit component for a result.\n259\t\n260\t        Uses semantic matching on component descriptions and\n261\t        historical usage patterns.\n262\t\n263\t        Args:\n264\t            result_type: The type of result (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n265\t            result_data: The structured result data\n266\t            layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use ('compact', 'normal', 'expanded')\n267\t\n268\t        Returns:\n269\t            The best matching Component, or None if no good match\n270\t        \"\"\"\n271\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n272\t\n273\t        # First, check usage patterns for this exact result type and layout\n274\t        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n275\t            \"\"\"\n276\t            SELECT component_id, match_score\n277\t            FROM component_usage_patterns\n278\t            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n279\t            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n280\t            LIMIT 1\n281\t            \"\"\",\n282\t            (result_type, layout_bucket)\n283\t        ).fetchone()\n284\t\n285\t        if pattern_row and pattern_row[1] >= 0.7:  # 70% confidence threshold\n286\t            component = self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n287\t            if component:\n288\t                return component\n289\t\n290\t        # Fallback: semantic search on component descriptions\n291\t        # (In production, this would use embeddings. For now, use simple keyword match)\n292\t        components = conn.execute(\n293\t            \"SELECT id, name, description FROM components WHERE usage_count > 0\"\n294\t        ).fetchall()\n295\t\n296\t        best_component = None\n297\t        best_score = 0.0\n298\t\n299\t        for comp_id, name, description in components:\n300\t            score = self._semantic_score(result_type, name, description)\n301\t            if score > best_score:\n302\t                best_score = score\n303\t                best_component = self.get_component(comp_id)\n304\t\n305\t        # Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n306\t        if best_score >= 0.5:\n307\t            return best_component\n308\t\n309\t        return None\n310\t\n311\t    def select_component_for_result_type(\n312\t        self,\n313\t        result_type: str,\n314\t        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n315\t        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n316\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n317\t        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n318\t\n319\t        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n320\t        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n321\t        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n322\t        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n323\t        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n324\t        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n325\t\n326\t        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n327\t        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n328\t        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n329\t        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n330\t\n331\t        Args:\n332\t            result_type: The result type to match against\n333\t            match_threshold: Minimum score threshold (default 0.7)\n334\t            layout_bucket: The layout bucket (default 'normal')\n335\t        \"\"\"\n336\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n337\t        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n338\t            \"\"\"\n339\t            SELECT component_id, match_score\n340\t            FROM component_usage_patterns\n341\t            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ? AND match_score >= ?\n342\t            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n343\t            LIMIT 1\n344\t            \"\"\",\n345\t            (result_type, layout_bucket, match_threshold),\n346\t        ).fetchone()\n347\t\n348\t        if not pattern_row:\n349\t            return None\n350\t\n351\t        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n352\t\n353\t    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n354\t        \"\"\"\n355\t        Calculate semantic match score.\n356\t\n357\t        Simple keyword-based scoring. In production, use embeddings.\n358\t        \"\"\"\n359\t        result_type_lower = result_type.lower()\n360\t        comp_name_lower = comp_name.lower()\n361\t        comp_desc_lower = comp_description.lower()\n362\t\n363\t        score = 0.0\n364\t\n365\t        # Exact name match\n366\t        if result_type_lower == comp_name_lower:\n367\t            return 1.0\n368\t\n369\t        # Name contains result type\n370\t        if result_type_lower in comp_name_lower:\n371\t            score += 0.7\n372\t\n373\t        # Description contains keywords from result type\n374\t        result_keywords = result_type_lower.replace('-', ' ').split()\n375\t        for keyword in result_keywords:\n376\t            if keyword in comp_desc_lower:\n377\t                score += 0.1\n378\t\n379\t        return min(score, 1.0)\n380\t\n381\t    def update_component(\n382\t        self,\n383\t        component_id: str,\n384\t        html_template: str,\n385\t        change_note: str\n386\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n387\t        \"\"\"\n388\t        Update a component to a new version.\n389\t\n390\t        Invalidates all cached cards using this component.\n391\t\n392\t        Args:\n393\t            component_id: The component to update\n394\t            html_template: The new template\n395\t            change_note: Why this change was made\n396\t\n397\t        Returns:\n398\t            The updated Component, or None if not found\n399\t        \"\"\"\n400\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n401\t\n402\t        # Get current version\n403\t        row = conn.execute(\n404\t            \"SELECT version FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n405\t            (component_id,)\n406\t        ).fetchone()\n407\t\n408\t        if not row:\n409\t            return None\n410\t\n411\t        new_version = row[0] + 1\n412\t        now = int(time.time())\n413\t\n414\t        # Update component\n415\t        conn.execute(\n416\t            \"\"\"\n417\t            UPDATE components\n418\t            SET html_template = ?, version = ?, last_used = ?\n419\t            WHERE id = ?\n420\t            \"\"\",\n421\t            (html_template, new_version, now, component_id)\n422\t        )\n423\t\n424\t        # Add version history\n425\t        conn.execute(\n426\t            \"\"\"\n427\t            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n428\t            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n429\t            \"\"\",\n430\t            (component_id, new_version, html_template, now, change_note)\n431\t        )\n432\t\n433\t        # Invalidate cached cards\n434\t        conn.execute(\n435\t            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE component_id = ?\",\n436\t            (component_id,)\n437\t        )\n438\t\n439\t        conn.commit()\n440\t\n441\t        return self.get_component(component_id)\n442\t\n443\t    def cache_card(\n444\t        self,\n445\t        result_id: str,\n446\t        component_id: str,\n447\t        component_version: int,\n448\t        layout_bucket: str,\n449\t        rendered_html: str\n450\t    ):\n451\t        \"\"\"\n452\t        Cache a rendered card.\n453\t\n454\t        Args:\n455\t            result_id: The result this card renders\n456\t            component_id: The component used\n457\t            component_version: The component version\n458\t            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n459\t            rendered_html: The rendered HTML\n460\t        \"\"\"\n461\t        if layout_bucket not in self.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n462\t            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid layout bucket: {layout_bucket}\")\n463\t\n464\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n465\t        conn.execute(\n466\t            \"\"\"\n467\t            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO card_cache\n468\t            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at)\n469\t            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n470\t            \"\"\",\n471\t            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, int(time.time()))\n472\t        )\n473\t        conn.commit()\n474\t\n475\t        #  match_score: float,\n528\t        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n529\t    ):\n530\t        \"\"\"\n531\t        Record a component usage pattern for future matching.\n532\t\n533\t ?)\n546\t            ON CONFLICT(result_type, component_id, layout_bucket)\n547\t            DO UPDATE SET\n548\t                match_score = (match_score * sample_count + ?) / (sample_count +        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Create component\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO components (id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, usage_count)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?, 0)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, name, description, html_template, created_at)\n        )\n\n        # Create version history\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, 1, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return Component(\n            id=component_id,\n            name=name,\n            description=description,\n            html_template=html_template,\n            version=1,\n            created_at=created_at,\n            last_used=None,\n            usage_count=0\n        )\n\n    def get_component(self, component_id: str) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Get a component by ID.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count \"\n            \"FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if row:\n            return Component(\n                id=row[0],\n                name=row[1],\n                description=row[2],\n                html_template=row[3],\n                version=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5],\n                last_used=row[6],\n                usage_count=row[7]\n            )\n        return None\n\n    def find_best_component(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: Dict[str, Any],\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal'\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Find the best-fit component for a result.\n\n        Uses semantic matching on component descriptions and\n        historical usage patterns.\n\n        Args:\n            result_type: The type of result (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n            result_data: The structured result data\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use ('compact', 'normal', 'expanded')\n\n        Returns:\n            The best matching Component, or None if no good match\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # First, check usage patterns for this exact result type and layout\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, layout_bucket)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if pattern_row and pattern_row[1] >= 0.7:  # 70% confidence threshold\n            component = self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n            if component:\n                return component\n\n        # Fallback: semantic search on component descriptions\n        # (In production, this would use embeddings. For now, use simple keyword match)\n        components = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT id, name, description FROM components WHERE usage_count > 0\"\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        best_component = None\n        best_score = 0.0\n\n        for comp_id, name, description in components:\n            score = self._semantic_score(result_type, name, description)\n            if score > best_score:\n                best_score = score\n                best_component = self.get_component(comp_id)\n\n        # Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n        if best_score >= 0.5:\n            return best_component\n\n        return None\n\n    def select_component_for_result_type(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n\n        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n\n        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n\n        Args:\n            result_type: The result type to match against\n            match_threshold: Minimum score threshold (default 0.7)\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket (default 'normal')\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ? AND match_score >= ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, layout_bucket, match_threshold),\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not pattern_row:\n            return None\n\n        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n\n    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Calculate semantic match score.\n\n        Simple keyword-based scoring. In production, use embeddings.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_type_lower = result_type.lower()\n        comp_name_lower = comp_name.lower()\n        comp_desc_lower = comp_description.lower()\n\n        score = 0.0\n\n        # Exact name match\n        if result_type_lower == comp_name_lower:\n            return 1.0\n\n        # Name contains result type\n        if result_type_lower in comp_name_lower:\n            score += 0.7\n\n        # Description contains keywords from result type\n        result_keywords = result_type_lower.replace('-', ' ').split()\n        for keyword in result_keywords:\n            if keyword in comp_desc_lower:\n                score += 0.1\n\n        return min(score, 1.0)\n\n    def update_component(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        html_template: str,\n        change_note: str\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update a component to a new version.\n\n        Invalidates all cached cards using this component.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to update\n            html_template: The new template\n            change_note: Why this change was made\n\n        Returns:\n            The updated Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get current version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT version FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        new_version = row[0] + 1\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        # Update component\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET html_template = ?, version = ?, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (html_template, new_version, now, component_id)\n        )\n\n        # Add version history\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, new_version, html_template, now, change_note)\n        )\n\n        # Invalidate cached cards\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE component_id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return self.get_component(component_id)\n\n    def cache_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        component_version: int,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n        rendered_html: str\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache a rendered card.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result this card renders\n            component_id: The component used\n            component_version: The component version\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n            rendered_html: The rendered HTML\n        \"\"\"\n        if layout_bucket not in self.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid layout bucket: {layout_bucket}\")\n\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO card_cache\n            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, int(time.time()))\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n        # Update component usage stats\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET usage_count = usage_count + 1, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (int(time.time()), component_id)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def get_cached_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        layout_bucket: str\n    ) -> Optional[CachedCard]:\n        \"\"\"Get a cached card if it exists and is up-to-date.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at\n            FROM card_cache\n            WHERE result_id = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if row:\n            return CachedCard(\n                result_id=row[0],\n                component_id=row[1],\n                component_version=row[2],\n                layout_bucket=row[3],\n                rendered_html=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5]\n            )\n        return None\n\n    def invalidate_result(self, result_id: str):\n        \"\"\"Invalidate all cached cards for a result.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE result_id = ?\",\n            (result_id,)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def record_usage_pattern(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_score: float,\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Record a component usage pattern for future matching.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component that was used\n            result_type: The type of result it rendered\n            match_score: How well it matched (0-1)\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket used (default 'normal')\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_usage_patterns (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?)\n            ON CONFLICT(result_type, component_id, layout_bucket)\n            DO UPDATE SET\n                match_score = (match_score * sample_count + ?) / (sample_count + 1),\n                sample_count = sample_count + 1,\n                updated_at = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, now, match_score, now)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def list_components(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Component]:\n        \"\"\"List all components, ordered by usage.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        rows = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count\n            FROM components\n            ORDER BY usage_count DESC, last_used DESC\n            LIMIT ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (limit,)\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        return [\n            Component(\n                id=row[0],\n                name=row[1],\n                description=row[2],\n                html_template=row[3],\n                version=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5],\n                last_used=row[6],\n                usage_count=row[7]\n            )\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n    def get_component_history(self, component_id: str) -> List[ComponentVersion]:\n        \"\"\"Get version history for a component.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        rows = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note\n            FROM component_versions\n            WHERE component_id = ?\n            ORDER BY version DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        return [\n            ComponentVersion(\n                component_id=row[0],\n                version=row[1],\n                html_template=row[2],\n                created_at=row[3],\n                change_note=row[4]\n            )\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n    def rollback_component(self, component_id: str, to_version: int) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback a component to a previous version.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to rollback\n            to_version: The version to rollback to\n\n        Returns:\n            The rolled-back Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get the target version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT html_template FROM component_versions\n            WHERE component_id = ? AND version = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, to_version)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        html_template = row[0]\n\n        # Rollback by updating with the old template as a new version\n        return self.update_component(\n            component_id,\n            html_template,\n            f\"Rollback to version {to_version}\"\n        )\n\n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the database connection.\"\"\"\n        if self._conn:\n            self._conn.close()\n            self._conn = None\n\n\n# Singleton instance for the application\n_library_instance: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None\n\n\ndef get_library(db_path: str = '/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/components.db') -> ComponentLibrary:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the component library singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _library_instance\n    if _library_instance is None:\n        _library_instance = ComponentLibrary(db_path)\n    return _library_instance\n","numLines":658,"startLine":1,"totalLines":658}}}

=============================================================================\n490\t# select_rendered_card Tests\n491\t# =============================================================================\n492\t\n493\tclass TestSelectRenderedCard:\n494\t    \"\"\"Test server-side deterministic component selector.\n495\t\n496\t    Tests cover the three acceptance criteria cases:\n497\t    1. Match case - component found with score >= threshold\n498\t    2. No-match case - no component found for result_type\n499\t    3. Below-threshold case - component found but score < threshold\n500\t    \"\"\"\n501\t\n502\t    def test_match_case_returns_rendered_html(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n503\t        \"\"\"TC-SRC-001: Match case - component found with score >= threshold returns rendered HTML.\n504\t\n505\t        When a component exists in component_usage_patterns with match_score >= threshold,\n506\t        select_rendered_card should:\n507\t        - Fill the component template with result.data\n508\t        - Write to card_cache\n509\t        - Return the rendered HTML string\n510\t        \"\"\"\n511\t        result_id = \"result-test-123\"\n512\t        result_type = \"status:test-project\"\n513\t\n514\t        # Record a high-confidence usage pattern (above threshold)\n515\t        component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n516\t            component_id=sample_component.id,\n517\t            result_type=result_type,\n518\t            match_score=0.85,  # Above 0.7 threshold\n519\t            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n520\t        )\n521\t\n522\t        # Call select_rendered_card\n523\t        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n524\t            result_type=result_type,\n525\t            result_data=sample_result_data,\n526\t            result_id=result_id,\n527\t            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n528\t            match_threshold=0.7,\n529\t            library=component_library\n530\t        )\n531\t\n532\t        # Assert: Should return rendered HTML (not None)\n533\t        assert rendered_html is not None\n534\t        assert isinstance(rendered_html, str)\n535\t        assert len(rendered_html) > 0\n536\t\n537\t        # Assert: Template should be filled with result data\n538\t        assert \"Test Status\" in rendered_html  # title from result_data\n539\t        assert \"All systems operational\" in rendered_html  # summary from result_data\n540\t        assert \"<div class=\\\"test-card\\\">\" in rendered_html  # template structure\n541\t\n542\t        # Assert: Should have written to card_cache\n543\t        cached_card = component_library.get_cached_card(\n544\t            result_id=result_id,\n545\t            component_id=sample_component.id,\n546\t            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n547\t        )\n548\t        assert cached_card is not None\n549\t        assert cached_card.rendered_html == rendered_html\n550\t\n551\t    def test_no_match_case_returns_none(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n552\t        \"\"\"TC-SRC-002: No-match case - no component found for result_type returns None.\n553\t\n554\t        When no component exists in component_usage_patterns for the given result_type,\n555\t        select_rendered_card should return None, triggering fallback rendering.\n556\t        \"\"\"\n557\t        result_id = \"result-test-456\"\n558\t        result_type = \"status:nonexistent-project\"\n559\t\n560\t        # Assert: Should return None (triggers fallback)\n573\t        assert rendered_html is None\n574\t\n575\t        # Assert: Should NOT have written to card_cache\n576\t        cached_card = component_library.get_cached_card(\n577\t            result_id=result_id,\n578\t            component_id=sample_component.id,\n579\t            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n580\t        )\n581\t        assert cached_card is None\n582\t\n583\t    def test_below_threshold_case_returns_none(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n584\t        \"\"\"TC-SRC-003: Below-threshold case - component found but score < threshold returns None.\n585\t\n586\t        When a component exists in component_usage_patterns but with match_score below threshold,\n587\t        select_rendered_card should return None, triggering fallback rendering.\n588\t        \"\"\"\n589\t        result_id = \"result-test-789\"\n590\t        result_type = \"status:low-confidence-project\"\n591\t\n592\t        # Exactly at threshold\n634\t            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n635\t        )\n636\t\n637\t        # Call select_rendered_card\n638\t        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n639\t            result_type=result_type,\n640\t            result_data=sample_result_data,\n641\t            result_id=result_id,\n642\t            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n643\t            match_threshold=0.7,\n644\t            library=component_library\n645\t        )\n646\t\n647\t        # Assert: Should return rendered HTML (threshold is inclusive)\n648\t        assert rendered_html is not None\n649\t        assert isinstance(rendered_html, str)\n650\t\n651\t    def test_records_usage_pattern_with_score_1_on_match(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n652\t        \"\"\"TC-SRC-005: On successful match, should record usage pattern with score=1.0.\n653\t\n654\t        Hot-path matches are high-confidence, so they should be recorded with match_score=1.0\n655\t        to bump reliable components toward the top of the selector.\n656\t        \"\"\"\n657\t        result_id = \"result-test-usage\"\n658\t        result_type = \"status:usage-tracking-project\"\n659\t\n660\t        # =============================================================================\n\nclass TestSelectRenderedCard:\n    \"\"\"Test server-side deterministic component selector.\n\n    Tests cover the three acceptance criteria cases:\n    1. Match case - component found with score >= threshold\n    2. No-match case - no component found for result_type\n    3. Below-threshold case - component found but score < threshold\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_match_case_returns_rendered_html(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n        \"\"\"TC-SRC-001: Match case - component found with score >= threshold returns rendered HTML.\n\n        When a component exists in component_usage_patterns with match_score >= threshold,\n        select_rendered_card should:\n        - Fill the component template with result.data\n        - Write to card_cache\n        - Return the rendered HTML string\n        \"\"\"\n        result_id = \"result-test-123\"\n        result_type = \"status:test-project\"\n\n        # Record a high-confidence usage pattern (above threshold)\n        component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            match_score=0.85,  # Above 0.7 threshold\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        # Call select_rendered_card\n        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n            result_type=result_type,\n            result_data=sample_result_data,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n            match_threshold=0.7,\n            library=component_library\n        )\n\n        # Assert: Should return rendered HTML (not None)\n        assert rendered_html is not None\n        assert isinstance(rendered_html, str)\n        assert len(rendered_html) > 0\n\n        # Assert: Template should be filled with result data\n        assert \"Test Status\" in rendered_html  # title from result_data\n        assert \"All systems operational\" in rendered_html  # summary from result_data\n        assert \"<div class=\\\"test-card\\\">\" in rendered_html  # template structure\n\n        # Assert: Should have written to card_cache\n        cached_card = component_library.get_cached_card(\n            result_id=result_id,\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n        assert cached_card is not None\n        assert cached_card.rendered_html == rendered_html\n\n    def test_no_match_case_returns_none(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n        \"\"\"TC-SRC-002: No-match case - no component found for result_type returns None.\n\n        When no component exists in component_usage_patterns for the given result_type,\n        select_rendered_card should return None, triggering fallback rendering.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_id = \"result-test-456\"\n        result_type = \"status:nonexistent-project\"\n\n        # Don't record any usage pattern - simulating no match\n\n        # Call select_rendered_card\n        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n            result_type=result_type,\n            result_data=sample_result_data,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n            match_threshold=0.7,\n            library=component_library\n        )\n\n        # Assert: Should return None (triggers fallback)\n        assert rendered_html is None\n\n        # Assert: Should NOT have written to card_cache\n        cached_card = component_library.get_cached_card(\n            result_id=result_id,\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n        assert cached_card is None\n\n    def test_below_threshold_case_returns_none(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n        \"\"\"TC-SRC-003: Below-threshold case - component found but score < threshold returns None.\n\n        When a component exists in component_usage_patterns but with match_score below threshold,\n        select_rendered_card should return None, triggering fallback rendering.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_id = \"result-test-789\"\n        result_type = \"status:low-confidence-project\"\n\n        # Record a low-confidence usage pattern (below threshold)\n        component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            match_score=0.65,  # Below 0.7 threshold\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        # Call select_rendered_card with threshold higher than recorded score\n        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n            result_type=result_type,\n            result_data=sample_result_data,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n            match_threshold=0.7,\n            library=component_library\n        )\n\n        # Assert: Should return None (triggers fallback)\n        assert rendered_html is None\n\n        # Assert: Should NOT have written to card_cache\n        cached_card = component_library.get_cached_card(\n            result_id=result_id,\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n        assert cached_card is None\n\n    def test_exactly_at_threshold_returns_html(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n        \"\"\"TC-SRC-004: Edge case - match_score exactly at threshold (0.7) should match.\n\n        When match_score equals the threshold exactly, it should be considered a match.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_id = \"result-test-threshold\"\n        result_type = \"status:threshold-project\"\n\n        # Record usage pattern with score exactly at threshold\n        component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            match_score=0.70,  # Exactly at threshold\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        # Call select_rendered_card\n        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n            result_type=result_type,\n            result_data=sample_result_data,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n            match_threshold=0.7,\n            library=component_library\n        )\n\n        # Assert: Should return rendered HTML (threshold is inclusive)\n        assert rendered_html is not None\n        assert isinstance(rendered_html, str)\n\n    def test_records_usage_pattern_with_score_1_on_match(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n        \"\"\"TC-SRC-005: On successful match, should record usage pattern with score=1.0.\n\n        Hot-path matches are high-confidence, so they should be recorded with match_score=1.0\n        to bump reliable components toward the top of the selector.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_id = \"result-test-usage\"\n        result_type = \"status:usage-tracking-project\"\n\n        # Record initial pattern with moderate score\n        component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            match_score=0.75,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        # Call select_rendered_card (should record new usage at 1.0)\n        rendered_html = select_rendered_card(\n            result_type=result_type,\n            result_data=sample_result_data,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n            match_threshold=0.7,\n            library=component_library\n        )\n\n        # Assert: Should have recorded the usage pattern\n        conn = component_library._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT match_score, sample_count\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, sample_component.id, \"normal\")\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        assert row is not None\n        # The match_score should have been updated toward 1.0 by the new recording\n        # (exact value depends on the running average calculation)\n        assert row[1] >= 2  # sample_count should have increased\n\n\nclass \"<script>alert('xss')</script>\"}\n        result = fill_template(template, data)\n        assert \"&lt;script&gt;\" in result\n        assert \"<script>\" not in result\n\n    def
